buyer_evidence_items.buyer_question: Can SecurityRecipes safely feed current, source-backed security context into model and agent products without leaking customer data?
buyer_evidence_items.required_artifact: Redacted tenant context package, lineage ledger, source freshness export, egress decision, and telemetry redaction sample.
buyer_evidence_items.title: Frontier lab context gateway diligence

buyer_evidence_items.buyer_question: Can a hosted service authorize MCP calls before execution and detect connector or tool-surface drift?
buyer_evidence_items.required_artifact: Protected-resource metadata, scope challenge trace, connector promotion record, drift decision, and pre-execution authorization receipt.
buyer_evidence_items.title: Agent runtime MCP gateway diligence

buyer_evidence_items.buyer_question: Can procurement, GRC, and security teams review evidence without exposing private prompts, source code, secrets, or regulated data?
buyer_evidence_items.required_artifact: Redacted trust-center export, data handling matrix, retention/deletion proof, telemetry schema, and zero secret-capture evidence.
buyer_evidence_items.title: Regulated enterprise trust-center diligence

buyer_evidence_items.buyer_question: Can agentic AI and MCP events produce detections that a SOC can route, triage, and contain?
buyer_evidence_items.required_artifact: SIEM query pack, sample alerts, containment decisions, token misuse detection, and incident replay evidence.
buyer_evidence_items.title: Security platform SOC wedge diligence

buyer_evidence_items.buyer_question: Can the hosted product become a real business, not only an impressive open corpus?
buyer_evidence_items.required_artifact: Usage meters, entitlement records, renewal metric export, support commitment, paid-wedge confirmation, and customer proof pack.
buyer_evidence_items.title: Acquirer revenue proof diligence

commercial_packaging.acquirer_value: A strategic buyer gets a secure context control plane that can attach to agent hosts, MCP gateways, AI trust centers, SOC pipelines, and private enterprise context without rebuilding the evidence model from scratch.

enterprise_plans.meter: context packages and read-only MCP decision evidence
enterprise_plans.proof_needed: One private context package, redacted telemetry, and reviewer outcome loop

enterprise_plans.meter: protected MCP decisions, connector namespaces, and trust-center exports
enterprise_plans.proof_needed: Authorization, drift, egress, telemetry, and receipt evidence across multiple connectors
enterprise_plans.target: AI platform and agent runtime teams

enterprise_plans.meter: tenant-bound evidence retention, SOC exports, and diligence packs
enterprise_plans.proof_needed: Deletion, retention, SLO, incident, and redacted trust-center evidence
enterprise_plans.target: Regulated enterprise and critical infrastructure programs

commercial_packaging.hosted_layer: Paid tenant-bound context gateway with protected MCP authorization, private context ingestion, connector trust, telemetry, signed receipts, SOC export, source freshness SLA, and customer proof retention.
commercial_packaging.open_layer: Free public site, generated evidence packs, local read-only MCP server, source freshness watch, and trust-center export for credibility and distribution.

evidence_sources.readiness_role: Defines protected-resource metadata, resource indicators, token audience, scope challenge, step-up authorization, session binding, and token-passthrough checks for hosted MCP.
evidence_sources.title: MCP Authorization Conformance Pack

evidence_sources.readiness_role: Keeps local subprocess launch risk explicit so hosted MCP does not inherit unsafe package-on-launch, ambient environment, or unreviewed command execution patterns.
evidence_sources.title: MCP STDIO Launch Boundary Pack

evidence_sources.readiness_role: Pins tool descriptions, schemas, annotations, and capability metadata before live hosted connectors are allowed to change behavior.
evidence_sources.title: MCP Tool Surface Drift Pack

evidence_sources.readiness_role: Supplies connector tiers, namespace boundaries, promotion criteria, kill signals, and workflow coverage for a hosted connector registry.
evidence_sources.title: MCP Connector Trust Pack

evidence_sources.readiness_role: Provides pre-promotion decisions, control gaps, registry patch previews, and red-team drills before a new hosted MCP connector reaches customers.
evidence_sources.title: MCP Connector Intake Pack

evidence_sources.readiness_role: Defines launch-review gates across autonomy, data, tools, memory, handoffs, telemetry, guardrails, and approval evidence for hosted agent customers.
evidence_sources.title: Agentic App Intake Pack

evidence_sources.readiness_role: Defines the receipt structure that hosted MCP needs for identity, context, tool decisions, egress, approvals, verifier output, and closure.
evidence_sources.title: Agentic Run Receipt Pack

evidence_sources.readiness_role: Defines metadata-first telemetry, redaction rules, runtime attributes, and evidence quality checks for customer-private hosted operations.
evidence_sources.title: Agentic Telemetry Contract

evidence_sources.readiness_role: Turns hosted MCP telemetry into SIEM-ready detections, response playbooks, and runtime alert decisions for agentic AI and connector abuse.
evidence_sources.title: Agentic SOC Detection Pack

evidence_sources.readiness_role: Defines tenant, model, telemetry, public corpus, DPA, residency, and secret-egress controls before private context leaves a boundary.
evidence_sources.title: Context Egress Boundary Pack

evidence_sources.readiness_role: Binds source hashes to retrieval, attestation, poisoning scans, model routes, egress decisions, handoffs, telemetry, and run receipts.
evidence_sources.title: Secure Context Lineage Ledger

evidence_sources.readiness_role: Proves that MCP, OWASP, NIST, CSA, OpenAI, CISA, and A2A guidance are watched before buyer-facing hosted claims are exported.
evidence_sources.title: Agentic Source Freshness Watch

evidence_sources.readiness_role: Bundles buyer-diligence evidence so hosted readiness gaps and proof claims can be shown through a trust-center export.
evidence_sources.title: Enterprise Trust Center Export

evidence_sources.readiness_role: Defines customer runtime events, metrics, renewal gates, and acquisition-proof blockers that the hosted product must collect.
evidence_sources.title: Secure Context Customer Proof Pack

next_90_days: Keep the public read-only MCP layer stable while adding hosted readiness evidence to the trust-center narrative.
Run one design-partner scoped pilot around private context ingestion, metadata-first telemetry, and customer-proof metrics.
Prototype protected-resource metadata, resource indicators, token audience validation, and no-token-passthrough enforcement for hosted MCP.
Define receipt verification semantics and customer-private export redaction before claiming acquisition-grade proof.
Package the first paid wedge around tenant-bound context packages, governed MCP decisions, and trust-center exports.

positioning.buyer: Frontier model labs, AI platform vendors, agent runtime teams, security platform vendors, regulated enterprises, VC diligence teams, and strategic acquirers
positioning.category: Hosted secure context and MCP readiness evidence
positioning.commercial_wedge: A hosted secure context gateway that sells tenant-bound context packages, MCP authorization evidence, source freshness, redacted run receipts, connector trust, and trust-center exports to teams deploying agents against private systems.
positioning.enterprise_promise: SecurityRecipes makes agentic AI easier to approve by showing exactly which hosted MCP controls must exist before private customer context, connector evidence, and runtime receipts can be trusted in production.
positioning.why_now: MCP, A2A, browser agents, persistent memory, tool marketplaces, and frontier agent products are creating a new control-plane category. Buyers need hosted infrastructure that turns open AI security knowledge into enforceable context, authorization, telemetry, and proof.

readiness_controls.buyer_claim: The public MCP server can safely return open SecurityRecipes context and generated evidence without customer-private write authority.
readiness_controls.title: Read-only reference MCP surface

readiness_controls.buyer_claim: Private context is never retrieved, cached, mixed, exported, or reused without tenant, source, package, egress, and receipt binding.
readiness_controls.title: Tenant-bound context index

readiness_controls.buyer_claim: Customer evidence can be ingested as hashes, metadata, and redacted summaries without leaking secrets, raw prompts, private code, or regulated data.
readiness_controls.title: Private evidence ingestion boundary

readiness_controls.buyer_claim: Hosted MCP calls are authorized before execution with resource indicators, audience validation, issuer checks, session binding, and explicit decisions.
readiness_controls.title: Protected-resource authorization

readiness_controls.buyer_claim: Agents start with minimal scope and must obtain explicit step-up authorization when a workflow needs more authority.
readiness_controls.title: Incremental scope and step-up challenges

readiness_controls.buyer_claim: The hosted service never accepts, transits, logs, or reuses user, model, or third-party tokens outside their intended resource boundary.
readiness_controls.title: No token passthrough

readiness_controls.buyer_claim: Hosted connectors run with namespace isolation, controlled egress, reviewed promotion criteria, and clear fallback when a connector cannot be trusted.
readiness_controls.title: Connector runtime isolation

readiness_controls.buyer_claim: Tool descriptions, schemas, annotations, and capabilities cannot silently drift into new authority or hidden instructions.
readiness_controls.title: Tool-surface drift monitoring

readiness_controls.buyer_claim: Sensitive third-party credentials and authorization flows are handled out of band on trusted domains, never through the MCP client prompt channel.
readiness_controls.title: URL-mode elicitation boundary

readiness_controls.buyer_claim: Customers can reconstruct agent and MCP behavior through metadata, hashes, decisions, and redacted summaries without raw secret capture.
readiness_controls.title: Metadata-first telemetry

readiness_controls.buyer_claim: Private context cannot leave a tenant, model, telemetry, public corpus, or external connector boundary without an explicit egress decision.
readiness_controls.title: Context egress enforcement

readiness_controls.buyer_claim: Every governed run can produce a tamper-evident receipt that links identity, context, authorization, egress, approvals, verifier output, and closure.
readiness_controls.title: Signed or verifiable run receipts

readiness_controls.buyer_claim: Agentic and MCP telemetry produces actionable SOC detections for token misuse, scope drift, tool drift, egress, prompt injection, and unsafe actions.
readiness_controls.title: SOC detection coverage

readiness_controls.buyer_claim: The hosted service can contain compromised connectors, leaked context, cross-tenant signals, or unsafe agent actions without waiting for a manual investigation.
readiness_controls.title: Incident containment runbooks

readiness_controls.buyer_claim: Tenant evidence, context packages, telemetry, and exports have explicit retention, deletion, tombstone, and re-export semantics.
readiness_controls.title: Retention and deletion controls

readiness_controls.buyer_claim: The hosted secure context layer has reliability, support, and recovery commitments that make it viable as enterprise infrastructure.
readiness_controls.title: Service SLO and disaster recovery

readiness_controls.buyer_claim: The hosted product can charge for context packages, governed MCP calls, connector namespaces, trust-center exports, and customer proof retention.
readiness_controls.title: Metering and entitlement

readiness_controls.buyer_claim: Buyer-facing hosted claims remain tied to monitored primary sources and generated pack regeneration paths.
readiness_controls.title: Source freshness SLA

readiness_controls.buyer_claim: A buyer can receive a redacted, source-backed, customer-specific trust-center export without exposing private context.
readiness_controls.title: Buyer proof export

readiness_controls.buyer_claim: The hosted product can prove safety and ROI with customer runtime metrics instead of relying on demo assumptions.
readiness_controls.title: Customer proof metrics

readiness_controls.buyer_claim: The open layer already produces a coherent trust-center export that can become customer-specific once private controls exist.
readiness_controls.title: Trust-center export

readiness_stages.blocked_until: Not blocked for public reference use; blocked for private customer context until tenant controls exist.
readiness_stages.commercial_value: Credible open layer that lets buyers inspect the corpus, evidence packs, and MCP surface before a hosted conversation.
readiness_stages.title: Open reference MCP server

readiness_stages.blocked_until: A design partner can provide private context roots, telemetry sinks, reviewer outcomes, and approved redaction boundaries.
readiness_stages.commercial_value: Turns the open corpus into customer proof by binding private context, receipts, telemetry, and reviewer outcomes for one scoped tenant.
readiness_stages.title: Design partner private context pilot

readiness_stages.blocked_until: Hosted authorization server, protected-resource metadata, tenant identity binding, connector isolation, and live tool-surface monitoring exist.
readiness_stages.commercial_value: Creates the enterprise product surface: a secure context gateway that can sit in front of agent hosts and live MCP connectors.
readiness_stages.title: Protected hosted MCP gateway

readiness_stages.blocked_until: Signed or verifiable receipts, SOC exports, incident containment, deletion workflows, support commitments, and disaster recovery objectives are implemented.
readiness_stages.commercial_value: Gives security, GRC, and procurement teams the operational assurance needed to treat the hosted service as enterprise infrastructure.
readiness_stages.title: Assurance and operations

readiness_stages.blocked_until: Metering, plan boundaries, private proof exports, support model, renewal metrics, and at least one paid wedge are validated by a design partner.
readiness_stages.commercial_value: Connects technical readiness to a credible paid wedge, expansion path, diligence export, and strategic acquisition story.
readiness_stages.title: Commercial packaging and acquisition readiness

risk_register.mitigation: Keep the readiness summary explicit: the open layer is ready for reference use, while tenant runtime controls, private ingestion, signing, and metering remain hosted gaps.
risk_register.risk: Buyers or contributors mistake the current read-only reference MCP server for a production tenant-safe hosted service.

risk_register.mitigation: Require tenant ids, context package hashes, egress decisions, lineage, retention policies, and deletion semantics before any private context pilot.
risk_register.risk: Private context, telemetry, receipts, or trust-center exports from one tenant become visible or reusable by another tenant.

risk_register.mitigation: Bind authorization to protected-resource metadata, resource indicators, token audience, issuer, session, scope challenge, and no-passthrough enforcement.
risk_register.risk: Hosted MCP accepts or forwards tokens that were not issued for the hosted resource, creating confused-deputy and credential leakage risk.

risk_register.mitigation: Pin tool-surface hashes, require connector intake, monitor capability expansion, and quarantine or disable changed connectors until reviewed.
risk_register.risk: A hosted connector changes descriptions, schemas, annotations, egress behavior, or hidden capabilities after approval.

risk_register.mitigation: Default to metadata, hashes, decisions, redacted summaries, and receipt ids; block proof counting when sensitive telemetry escape is detected.
risk_register.risk: Telemetry collected for proof captures prompts, secrets, private code, raw tool outputs, or regulated customer data.

risk_register.mitigation: Require receipt hashes, signing key ids or equivalent verification status, closure state, and source-pack hashes before acquisition-grade claims.
risk_register.risk: Run receipts can be edited after the fact, weakening buyer diligence, incident response, and acquisition proof.

risk_register.mitigation: Define meters for context packages, governed MCP decisions, connector namespaces, trust-center exports, evidence retention, and customer proof metrics.
risk_register.risk: The project creates technical credibility but no paid product surface that maps to budget owner, usage, renewal, or expansion.

rollout_gates.title: Tenant isolation gate
rollout_gates.what_passes: Tenant ids, context package hashes, egress decisions, retention policies, and deletion paths are enforced in runtime tests.

rollout_gates.title: Protected-resource authorization gate
rollout_gates.what_passes: Every protected hosted MCP call validates resource indicators, audience, issuer, session, scope, and token handling before execution.

rollout_gates.title: Connector promotion gate
rollout_gates.what_passes: A connector has approved namespace, sandbox, egress, tool-surface hashes, elicitation boundaries, and rollback behavior.

rollout_gates.title: Telemetry redaction gate
rollout_gates.what_passes: Telemetry reconstructs agent runs through metadata and decisions while secret, prompt, raw code, and regulated-data escape count stays zero.

rollout_gates.title: Receipt integrity gate
rollout_gates.what_passes: Receipts are tamper-evident or externally verifiable and bind identity, context, authorization, egress, approvals, telemetry, and closure.

rollout_gates.title: Operations gate
rollout_gates.what_passes: Incident containment, customer support, monitoring, SLOs, recovery targets, and emergency connector disablement are tested.

rollout_gates.title: Commercial gate
rollout_gates.what_passes: A design partner validates plan boundaries, usage meters, renewal metrics, and one budget-owned paid wedge.

source_references.name: Model Context Protocol basic specification
source_references.why_it_matters: The hosted plan must respect MCP's base protocol, lifecycle, resources, prompts, tools, client features, utilities, and transport separation instead of treating MCP as a generic REST wrapper.

source_references.name: Model Context Protocol Authorization
source_references.why_it_matters: Protected hosted MCP must bind tokens to resources, validate token audiences, support protected-resource metadata, minimize scopes, handle step-up authorization, and reject token passthrough.

source_references.name: Model Context Protocol Elicitation
source_references.why_it_matters: Hosted MCP needs safe user interaction boundaries, especially URL-mode flows where sensitive credentials or third-party authorization must not pass through the MCP client.

source_references.name: OpenTelemetry Semantic Conventions for MCP
source_references.why_it_matters: MCP spans, sessions, transports, methods, tool calls, errors, and duration metrics are the likely shared evidence language for customer proof, support, SOC detection, and SLOs.

source_references.name: OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications
source_references.why_it_matters: The hosted product must address agent behavior hijacking, tool misuse, identity abuse, unsafe autonomy, and evidence gaps as operational risks, not only documentation topics.

source_references.name: OWASP MCP Top 10
source_references.why_it_matters: MCP-specific risks such as token exposure, scope creep, tool poisoning, command execution, telemetry gaps, shadow servers, and context over-sharing define the hosted control surface.

source_references.name: AI Controls Matrix
source_references.why_it_matters: CSA's AI-specific control objectives and mappings give enterprise buyers a familiar assurance language for hosted AI control-plane adoption.

source_references.name: Capabilities-Based Risk Assessment for Agentic AI
source_references.why_it_matters: System criticality, autonomy, access permissions, and impact radius map directly to hosted tenant isolation, runtime approvals, and paid risk scoring.

source_references.name: NIST AI Agent Standards Initiative
source_references.why_it_matters: NIST's agent standards initiative validates the need for secure, interoperable agent infrastructure and gives the hosted MCP plan a government-aligned standards lane.

source_references.name: CAISI RFI on Securing AI Agent Systems
source_references.why_it_matters: The RFI highlights indirect prompt injection, poisoning, misaligned actions, access constraints, monitoring, and measurement as distinct agent-system security concerns.

source_references.name: AI Data Security: Best Practices for Securing Data Used to Train and Operate AI Systems
source_references.publisher: CISA, NSA, FBI, and international partners
source_references.why_it_matters: Customer-private context and evidence ingestion must preserve data accuracy, integrity, confidentiality, monitoring, detection, and lifecycle protections.

source_references.name: Joint Guidance on Deploying AI Systems Securely
source_references.publisher: CISA, NSA, FBI, ASD, CCCS, NCSC-NZ, and NCSC-UK
source_references.why_it_matters: Hosted MCP deployment must protect confidentiality, integrity, availability, vulnerability management, detection, response, and related AI system data.
